question: Design a `Course` class and a `Student` class. `Course` should have a `name` and a private pointer to a `Student` object (`Student* assignedStudent`) to demonstrate aggregation (a student can exist independently of a course). Implement methods to assign and unassign a student to the course.

test_cases.expected:
[DEBUG_INFO] A student object with name 'Alice Smith' has been successfully constructed.
[DEBUG_INFO] A student object with name 'Bob Johnson' has been successfully constructed.
[DEBUG_INFO] Course object with label 'Advanced Algorithms' has been initialized, with no student assigned initially.

--- Initial Course State ---
Course Identifier: Advanced Algorithms || Current Assigned Student Status: No Student Currently Assigned (Inactive Enrollment) || End of Details.

--- Performing enrollment for Alice ---
[DEBUG_INFO] Student ('Alice Smith') has been successfully assigned to course ('Advanced Algorithms').
Course Identifier: Advanced Algorithms || Current Assigned Student Status: Alice Smith (Active Enrollment) || End of Details.

--- Performing enrollment for Bob (replaces Alice) ---
[DEBUG_INFO] An existing student ('Alice Smith') was previously enrolled in course 'Advanced Algorithms'. Initiating unenrollment before new assignment.
[DEBUG_INFO] Student ('Bob Johnson') has been successfully assigned to course ('Advanced Algorithms').
Course Identifier: Advanced Algorithms || Current Assigned Student Status: Bob Johnson (Active Enrollment) || End of Details.

--- Executing unenrollment process ---
[DEBUG_INFO] Student ('Bob Johnson') is now being unassigned from course ('Advanced Algorithms').
Course Identifier: Advanced Algorithms || Current Assigned Student Status: No Student Currently Assigned (Inactive Enrollment) || End of Details.

--- Executing unenrollment process again (no student) ---
[DEBUG_INFO] No student was found to be assigned to course ('Advanced Algorithms'), so no unenrollment action was necessary.
Course Identifier: Advanced Algorithms || Current Assigned Student Status: No Student Currently Assigned (Inactive Enrollment) || End of Details.

--- Attempting enrollment with a null pointer ---
[DEBUG_INFO] Attempted to perform student enrollment operation with a null student pointer. No actual student was assigned.
Course Identifier: Advanced Algorithms || Current Assigned Student Status: No Student Currently Assigned (Inactive Enrollment) || End of Details.
[DEBUG_INFO] Course object with label 'Advanced Algorithms' is being destructed.

errors: Overly verbose class, member, and method names (e.g., `m_student_identification_name_string`, `perform_student_enrollment_operation`) significantly reduce readability.
Unnecessary `std::cout` debug statements are present in constructors and methods, cluttering output and potentially impacting performance.

The `perform_student_enrollment_operation` method performs a redundant unassignment if a student is already assigned, even if it's the same student or simply replacing an existing one, leading to inefficient operations.

The `obtain_assigned_student_details_string()` method builds a string using `std::ostringstream` for a simple output, which is less efficient than direct `std::cout` for this use case and adds unnecessary complexity.
